segments.source_start: One day he went up to the upper terrace of his mansion and sitting cross-legged in solitude, he thought (Bv 2.7-27):
segments.source_end: One day he went up to the upper terrace of his mansion and sitting cross-legged in solitude, he thought (Bv 2.7-27):
segments.notes: Normalized internal double space in raw: 'One day  he' -> 'One day he'.

segments.source_start: “Miserable, is birth in a new existence; so is destruction of the body; miserable, also it is to die in delusion, oppressed and overpowered by old age.
segments.source_end: Being subject to birth, old age and sickness, I will seek Nibbāna where old age, death and fear are extinct.
segments.notes: Paragraph preserved as a single quoted unit.

segments.source_start: Wonderful it would be, if I could abandon this body of mine without any regard for it, as it is full of putrid things, such as urine, excreta, pus, blood, bile, phlegm, saliva, mucus, etc.
segments.source_end: Wonderful it would be, if I could abandon this body of mine without any regard for it, as it is full of putrid things, such as urine, excreta, pus, blood, bile, phlegm, saliva, mucus, etc.
segments.notes: Raw file ends here; possible truncation noted in agent log.
